title: Black globe background + ensure all stations load including currently playing

requirements.text: Change the GlobeView and FullscreenGlobe component backgrounds from their current color to solid black (#000000) so the globe matches the rest of the app's dark background, while preserving all existing globe styling, markers, rings, and orbit controls.

quotes: keep the globe style, but make the bg black like the rest of the app

acceptanceCriteria: The Three.js scene background in GlobeView is set to black
The Three.js scene background in FullscreenGlobe is set to black
Any canvas or container wrapper background behind the globe is also black
All existing markers, rings, Earth texture, and orbit controls remain unchanged

requirements.text: Ensure the globe view loads and displays all available stations without missing entries. The currently playing station must always appear as a marker on the globe — if it is not already in the fetched globe station list, it must be added to the rendered markers regardless.

quotes: it's still missing some stations, it should load the station playing especially

acceptanceCriteria: The currently playing station is always rendered as a marker on the globe even if the API did not return it in the globe station list
The globe fetches the maximum number of stations supported (up to 500) to minimize missing entries
American stations that have valid geo coordinates appear on the globe
No station with valid lat/lng is silently dropped from the rendered markers

constraints: Do not alter the globe's Earth texture, marker design, pulsing ring animation, or orbit controls
Do not modify files listed in frontend.immutablePaths
nonGoals: Redesigning the globe visual style beyond background color
Changing backend station data or favorites logic
